query: Evaluate this skill — does it actually trigger and improve the output?
How do I test whether my new skill fires on the right prompts and stays quiet otherwise?
Measure my skill's performance — recall, specificity, correct-usage, the works.
Build a behavioral eval for the journaling skill so I can catch regressions.
Is my skill's description triggering correctly, or is it too broad?
Benchmark this skill against the no-skill baseline.
I changed a skill's description — verify I didn't break its triggering before I ship it.
Set up a scorecard that measures whether my skills activate and help.
Review my skill's design — is it bloated, redundant, or wrong?
note: near-miss: qualitative design review (review-panel), not behavioral measurement

query: Write a new Claude Code skill from scratch for me.
note: near-miss: authoring a skill, not evaluating one

query: Evaluate which Python web framework I should choose for this project.
note: near-miss: 'evaluate' but a tech choice, not a skill

query: Run the pytest suite for my Python package.
note: near-miss: running code tests, not skill behavior

query: How well does this model do on the MMLU benchmark?
note: near-miss: model benchmarking, not a skill

query: What skills and commands do I have installed in Claude Code?
Measure the p99 latency of my API endpoint under load.
Grade these student essays out of 100.
